economic_notes: Four government business enterprises accounted for by modified equity method: Driftpile First Nation Enterprises Inc. (100%), Driftpile Business Corporation (100%), Driftpile Construction Services Limited Partnership (99%), Driftpile Timber Management Limited Partnership (99%). Combined GBE revenue $7,982,774; net loss $(364,130). COVID-19 pandemic disclosed as significant uncertainty.
notes: Four GBEs accounted for by modified equity method (not consolidated line by line). Fiscal year 2020. Large increase in ISC funding year over year ($15.8M to $27M) and significant capital asset additions ($15.3M). Public Works budget vs actual variance is extreme ($13.5M budget vs $2.1M actual), with corresponding large surplus in that segment. No schedule of remuneration disclosed. Restricted cash includes CMHC ($178,840) and First Nation Development Funds ($202,875). Ottawa Trust Funds $1,186,680 held by Government of Canada. Deferred revenue increased significantly to $5.4M. Long-term debt nearly doubled to $8.46M primarily due to ATB housing loans. COVID-19 uncertainty disclosed. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $27,048,770 + LSLIRC $664,452 + CMHC $61,840 = $27,774,062; FNDF $640,194 treated as government transfer making total $28,414,456 but FNDF classified separately so using ISC+LSLIRC+CMHC only for total_government_transfers.
warnings: High ISC dependency (82.22%) — operations heavily reliant on federal transfers
